title: Why Prayers Seem Unanswered: 7 Checks
hook: Prayer feels unheard? Check these 7 Bible reasons.
script: Prayer feels unheard? Check these 7 Bible reasons. Wrong motives? Ask God to purify your ask (James 4:3). Unforgiveness? Release them and then pray (Mark 11:25). Hidden sin? Confess and turn; God listens to the honest (Psalm 66:18). Different timing? Wait; the vision won’t lie (Habakkuk 2:3). Spiritual warfare? Keep praying; help may be delayed, not denied (Daniel 10:12-13). Gave up too soon? Pray and don’t lose heart (Luke 18:1-8). God’s better grace? He may give strength, not subtraction (2 Corinthians 12:9). Ask according to His will and take heart—He hears you (1 John 5:14-15).

title: Prayer Reset: From Silence to Confidence
hook: If you're tired of waiting on God, listen to this.
script: If you're tired of waiting on God, listen to this. Breathe and invite the Spirit. Check motives; ask for a clean desire (James 4:3). Forgive anyone as you pray (Mark 11:25). Confess what you’ve been hiding (Psalm 66:18). Submit the clock to God’s timing (Habakkuk 2:3). Ask for angelic help and protection in the unseen fight (Daniel 10). Return again, and again; persistence is faith in motion (Luke 18:1-8). Finally, welcome God’s better answer—sufficient grace (2 Corinthians 12:9). Now pray with confidence: if it’s His will, He hears and will act (1 John 5:14-15).

title: When Heaven Feels Quiet: 7 Alignment Questions
hook: If God feels silent right now, watch this.
script: If God feels silent right now, watch this. Seven questions to realign your prayer. Am I asking to spend it on myself? Lord, refine my motives (James 4:3). Am I holding a grudge? I forgive by faith (Mark 11:25). Is there hidden sin? I confess and turn (Psalm 66:18). Is it simply not time yet? I will wait (Habakkuk 2:3). Is there warfare? Strengthen and send help (Daniel 10). Have I stopped knocking? I’ll persist (Luke 18:1-8). Could Your no be a better yes—grace? Be strong in me (2 Corinthians 12:9). This is our confidence: He hears according to His will (1 John 5:14-15).
